title: Data Leakage pada Pemisahan Graf
scope: Why graph splits are subtle (connected nodes split naively across train/test).
key_concepts: Splitting a graph is fundamentally different from splitting tabular data because edges cross the train/test boundary.
If node A is in the training set and its connected neighbor node B is in the test set, information leaks. A GNN or neighborhood aggregation will use A's label or features to predict B.
Transductive vs Inductive learning: Transductive sees the whole graph topology during training (labels masked for test). Inductive splits the graph into entirely disconnected subgraphs.
Applying 'praktik pipeline yang benar' on graphs often requires careful temporal splits or subgraph isolation to prevent test-set features from bleeding into the training process.

definitions.meaning: Skenario di mana struktur graf secara keseluruhan (termasuk simpul uji) terlihat selama pelatihan, namun label untuk simpul uji disembunyikan.
Skenario di mana model dievaluasi pada graf atau subgraf yang benar-benar tidak terlihat selama fase pelatihan.

examples: If a fraud detection GNN trains on a network where known fraudsters are directly linked to test nodes, the message passing will leak the fraud signal perfectly to the test set, artificially boosting accuracy.
exclude: Deep dive into semi-supervised learning bounds.
relations: The graph equivalent of s03. Ties back to Ch 2's core message on pipeline discipline.
